claim: JCVI-Syn3A has approximately 500 ribosomes per cell at birth (newly divided, early cell cycle).
source_detail: Thornburg 2026 initialises the 4DWCM with approximately 500 ribosomes per cell at birth, consistent with cryo-electron tomography counts of Syn3A ribosomes (uniformly distributed throughout the cytoplasm). Precursor source: Bianchi 2022 quantitative proteomics + cryo-ET.
caveats: Approximate value (~500); actual measurement has cell-to-cell variability + measurement error from cryo-ET counting.
Thornburg 2026's simulation grows the ribosome pool over the cycle via ribosome-biogenesis kinetics; 500 is the initial-state value only.
Extracted from WebSearch snippets in Session 11.
